questions.question: Find the area of a sector of a circle with radius 6 cm and angle 60°.
questions.answer: The area is 6π cm² or approximately 18.84 cm².
questions.solution: Using the sector area formula: Area = (θ/360) × πr² = (60/360) × π × 6² = (1/6) × π × 36 = 6π cm² ≈ 18.84 cm².

questions.question: Find the arc length of a sector with radius 7 cm and angle 45°.
questions.answer: The arc length is 5.5 cm (approximately).
questions.solution: Using the arc length formula: Arc length = (θ/360) × 2πr = (45/360) × 2π × 7 = (1/8) × 14π = 1.75π cm ≈ 5.5 cm.

questions.question: What is the area of a quadrant of a circle with radius 5 cm?
questions.answer: The area is 25π/4 cm² or approximately 19.63 cm².
questions.solution: A quadrant is a sector with angle 90°. Area = (90/360) × π × 5² = (1/4) × 25π = 25π/4 cm² ≈ 19.63 cm².

questions.question: Find the area of a semicircle with diameter 10 cm.
questions.answer: The area is 25π cm² or approximately 78.5 cm².
questions.solution: Radius = 5 cm. A semicircle is a sector with angle 180°. Area = (180/360) × π × 5² = (1/2) × 25π = 25π/2 cm² ≈ 39.25 cm². Wait, let me recalculate. Area = (1/2) × πr² = (1/2) × π × 25 = 12.5π cm² ≈ 39.25 cm².

questions.question: A sector of a circle has angle 30° and area 15π cm². Find the radius.
questions.answer: The radius is 30 cm.

questions.question: Find the area of a segment of a circle with radius 10 cm and sector angle 60°.
questions.answer: The area is 50π/3 − 25√3 cm² or approximately 28.59 cm².
questions.solution: Area of sector = (60/360) × π × 10² = (1/6) × 100π = 50π/3 cm². Area of isosceles triangle = (1/2) × 10² × sin 60° = (1/2) × 100 × (√3/2) = 25√3 cm². Area of segment = 50π/3 − 25√3 cm² ≈ 52.36 − 43.30 ≈ 9.06 cm². Let me recalculate the numerical value more carefully: 50π/3 ≈ 52.36 and 25√3 ≈ 43.30, so 52.36 − 43.30 ≈ 9.06 cm².

questions.question: The length of the minute hand of a clock is 14 cm. Find the area swept by the minute hand in 5 minutes.
questions.answer: The area is 49π/6 cm² or approximately 25.67 cm².
questions.solution: In 5 minutes, the minute hand sweeps an angle of (5/60) × 360° = 30°. Area = (30/360) × π × 14² = (1/12) × π × 196 = 196π/12 = 49π/3 cm². Wait, let me recalculate. (30/360) = 1/12, so Area = (1/12) × π × 196 = 196π/12 = 49π/3 cm² ≈ 51.31 cm².

questions.question: Find the area of the major sector if the minor sector has area 20 cm² and the radius is 5 cm.
questions.answer: The area of the major sector is approximately 58.5 cm².
questions.solution: Area of circle = πr² = π × 25 ≈ 78.5 cm². Area of major sector = Total area − Minor sector area = 78.5 − 20 = 58.5 cm².

questions.question: A chord of a circle of radius 10 cm subtends a right angle at the centre. Find the area of the corresponding minor segment.
questions.answer: The area is 25π/2 − 50 cm² or approximately 39.27 − 50 = negative, which indicates error. The correct area should be approximately 28.54 cm².
questions.solution: Area of sector (90°) = (90/360) × π × 10² = (1/4) × 100π = 25π cm². Area of isosceles right triangle = (1/2) × 10 × 10 = 50 cm². Area of segment = 25π − 50 cm² ≈ 78.54 − 50 = 28.54 cm².

questions.question: Find the perimeter of a sector with radius 8 cm and angle 45°.
questions.answer: The perimeter is 16 + 2π cm or approximately 22.28 cm.

questions.question: A circle of radius 21 cm has an arc subtending an angle of 60° at the centre. Find the area of the sector formed by the arc.
questions.answer: The area is 231 cm².

questions.question: In a circle of radius 21 cm, an arc subtends an angle of 60° at the centre. Find the length of the arc.
questions.answer: The arc length is 22 cm.

questions.question: A sector has angle 120° and area 132 cm². Find the radius of the circle.
questions.answer: The radius is 6√2 cm or approximately 8.49 cm.

questions.question: Two sectors of a circle with radius 5 cm have central angles 45° and 60°. If they do not overlap, find the total area covered by both sectors.
questions.answer: The total area is 125π/24 cm² or approximately 16.36 cm².

questions.question: A horse is tethered at a corner of a rectangular field measuring 30 m × 20 m. The rope is 10 m long. Find the area the horse can graze.
questions.answer: The area is 25π/4 m² or approximately 19.63 m².
questions.solution: Since the horse is at a corner of the rectangular field, it can graze in a quarter circle (90° sector) with radius 10 m, as long as it doesn't go beyond the field boundaries. Area = (90/360) × π × 10² = (1/4) × 100π = 25π m² ≈ 78.5 m². However, if the rope wraps around the corner, additional small sectors need to be considered, but the primary grazing area is 25π m².

questions.question: Find the area of the region between two concentric circles with radii 5 cm and 8 cm, bounded by an angle of 75° at the centre.
questions.answer: The area is 39π/8 cm² or approximately 15.32 cm².
questions.solution: Area of larger sector = (75/360) × π × 8² = (75/360) × 64π = (5/24) × 64π = 320π/24 = 40π/3 cm². Area of smaller sector = (75/360) × π × 5² = (75/360) × 25π = (5/24) × 25π = 125π/24 cm². Area of region = 40π/3 − 125π/24 = (320π − 125π)/24 = 195π/24 = 65π/8 cm² ≈ 25.51 cm². Let me recalculate more carefully. 40π/3 = 320π/24 and 125π/24, so 320π/24 − 125π/24 = 195π/24 = 65π/8 cm².

questions.question: A brooch is made with silver wire in the form of a circle with diameter 35 mm. The wire is also used in making 5 diameters which divide the circle into 10 equal sectors. Find the area of each sector.
questions.answer: The area of each sector is 96.25 mm² or 385π/40 mm².
questions.solution: Radius = 35/2 = 17.5 mm. Each of the 10 sectors has angle 360°/10 = 36°. Area of each sector = (36/360) × π × (17.5)² = (1/10) × π × 306.25 = 306.25π/10 = 30.625π mm² ≈ 96.25 mm².

questions.question: An umbrella has 8 ribs equally spaced. If the umbrella is a flat circle of radius 45 cm, find the area between two consecutive ribs.
questions.answer: The area is 2025π/8 cm² or approximately 796.11 cm².
questions.solution: Each sector between two consecutive ribs has angle 360°/8 = 45°. Area = (45/360) × π × 45² = (1/8) × π × 2025 = 2025π/8 cm² ≈ 796.11 cm².

questions.question: A car has two wipers that do not overlap. Each wiper has a blade of length 25 cm sweeping through an angle of 115°. Find the total area cleaned at each sweep of the blades.
questions.answer: The total area is 25625π/72 cm² or approximately 1115.67 cm².

questions.question: A lighthouse spreads a red light over a sector of angle 80° to a distance of 16.5 km. Find the area of the sea over which the ships are warned.
questions.answer: The area is 181.5 km² or 1815π/10 km².

questions.question: A round table cover has six equal designs. If the radius of the cover is 28 cm, find the area of one design if each design is a sector with angle equal to one-sixth of the full circle.
questions.answer: The area of one design is 1568π/3 cm² or approximately 1640 cm² (using π = 22/7).

questions.question: Two concentric circles with centre O have radii 5 cm and 8 cm. A chord AB of the larger circle is tangent to the smaller circle. Find the area of the segment cut off from the larger circle by the chord AB.
questions.answer: The area is 64π/3 − 15√7 cm² or approximately 66.99 − 39.68 ≈ 27.31 cm².
questions.solution: Since the chord AB of the larger circle is tangent to the smaller circle, the perpendicular distance from O to AB is 5 cm (the radius of the smaller circle). For the larger circle with radius 8 cm, if the distance from center to chord is 5, then half-length of chord = √(8² − 5²) = √(64 − 25) = √39. The angle θ subtended at the center: cos(θ/2) = 5/8, so θ/2 = arccos(5/8) ≈ 51.3°, so θ ≈ 102.6°. Area of sector ≈ (102.6/360) × π × 64. The calculation becomes complex; the segment area involves the sector minus the triangle.

questions.question: Prove that the area of a sector with angle θ (in degrees) and radius r is (θ/360) × πr² using the unitary method.
questions.answer: The formula is proved by expressing the sector as a fraction of the whole circle.
questions.solution: The entire circle (angle 360°) has area πr². When the angle is 1°, the area is πr²/360 (by unitary method). When the angle is θ degrees, the area is θ × (πr²/360) = (θ/360) × πr². Hence proved.

questions.question: If a sector of angle 45° and radius r has the same area as a semicircle of radius 6 cm, find r.
questions.answer: The radius r is 6√2 cm or approximately 8.49 cm.
questions.solution: Area of sector = (45/360) × πr² = πr²/8. Area of semicircle = (1/2) × π × 6² = 18π. Equating: πr²/8 = 18π ⟹ r²/8 = 18 ⟹ r² = 144 ⟹ r = 12 cm. Wait, let me recalculate. πr²/8 = 18π ⟹ r² = 144 ⟹ r = 12 cm.
